column4: This file should be populated with ALL records that the EDI convertor received in the 834 file from one or more sources, and populate the status of each record to denote if they are  succesfully converted to XML and transmitted to HPSCIL/HRP. PULSE360 application needs this information to identify the fallout of records that happened within EDI convertor( eg:edifecs ) and thus to balance the inbound and outbound population within the ecosystem.

UST_<Layout Converter Name>_EDIextract_Enrollment_<Healthplan>_<Frequency>_<generatedTimeStamp>.csv
Example: UST_Edifecs_EDIextract_Enrollment_Highmark_Daily_12082021144200.csv
csv
Description
column10: Comments

column4:
Name of the Electronic health data exchange tool which recieves the enrollment data from multiple sources and convert them to xml format
Example - Edifecs/Mercator/SMART etc
column10:
Must be populated to Identify the EDI Converter involved with 834 for each customer.
Example : Edifecs ,Mercator etc

column4: The name of EDI file in which the convertor received 834 data from source
column10:
This is a Must have field for PULSE to calculate the transaction requests per file per day.
This filename should help to segregate the list of distinct files received from source and hence it is recommended to add created/received timestamp in the name for uniqueness of file name.

column4: Frequency of the file
column10: Must be populated with the source file frequency.

column4: Source from which the EDI file is received
column10: The Source from which this data has received . This is a Must have field for PULSE to calculate the total count of Enrollment transactions for each source

column4: Unique ID for each File generated by the EDI converter
column10: This is a Must have identifier that is required to identify each file uniquely.Should be the same file identifier that is sent to CIL/HRP in XML file

column4: Unique Record ID Generated by the EDI Converter to uniquely identify each transaction
column9: recordId in XML (This field has to be the same as 834 XML mapping enrollmentSparseSubmitRequestRecord/recordId)
column10: This field is a Must have field to identify each transaction uniquely. This is needed to keep track of multiple requests per member.Should be the same identifier that is sent to CIL/HRP

column4: Member identifier for which the Enrollment request is received from source
column9: REF02, where REF01=0F after the INS segment, which is the medicaid id
column10: Key Identifier which defines a member identity that helps to track each Transaction request for the member (end to end). This is a Must have field for PULSE

column4: Type of Enrollment request received from source for each member record.

'A'  - For a new member Addition
'C' - For the change request from member
 'T' - For the Termination request from member

This field is a Must have and has a critical impact to the PULSE dashboard.

column4: Accepted/Rejected/On-Hold/Other status (if any)
column8: A or R or H or O
column9:
A' - Accepted -For good records that is pushed to HRP/HPSCIL
'R' - Rejected - For bad records / For completely rejected file
'H' - OnHold - For records under review by the Vendor
'O' - Other
column10:
A' - Accepted -For good records that is pushed to HRP/HPSCIL
'R' - Rejected - For bad records / For completely rejected file
'H' - OnHold - For records under review by the Vendor
'O' - Other

This field help to categorise the records into different Data check points for the balancing purpose. Major field which is a Must have for the PULSE Dashboard to perform Balancing

column4: Rejection Code , if the record is not succesfully converted into XML and sent to HRP
column10: This field should be blank as per our new requirement provided on May 2024.

column4: Rejection Code & Reason for Rejection, if the record is not succesfully converted into XML and sent to HRP
column10:
1)	First 40 Encountered Message should be sent in Pulse CSV file. If there is more than 40 errors  for a transaction, then add a ellipsis ( ... ) to the end of 40th Error message (This is to understand there is more than 40 errors in a transaction).
2)	Error Code & Error Message field should be populated in same field (Ex:- Error Code : Error Description)
3)	To Differentiate the errors, || Two Pipe Delimited symbol should be placed between the Errors in order to identify and separate it out.
4)	Single Error Description limit should be restricted to 150 and if it is exceeds, then truncate and provide only the first 150 characters and add ellipsis ( ... ) to an end of the error message. (This is to understand the error message contains more than 150 characters).

column4: IF an entire file is rejected then the reason for rejection
column10:
If the entire file is rejected, the reason for the file rejection is needed to be displayed in the PULSE dashboard.

This field must be populated only if the entire file is rejected. For succesful files , this field can be Blank or NULL and record level rejections can be populated in Error_code and Error_message fields.

column4: Total number of errors tagged to this transaction by the EDI layout convertor
column10: To be populated if this record is rejected with errors. Number of errors will be displayed in PULSE dashboard and is a must have field for rejected records.
